verdicts.note: Score is airtight (five-year incumbency, vice-chairmanship, caucus chair, enacted bill, all cited). Flagging the highlights instead: the endorsements highlight cites only [S8] (Seattle Times), but WSLC is S10, King County Democrats is S9, and Ferguson/Brown come from the pamphlet S1. The claims are all true per the dossier but the single citation misattributes them.

No directional evidence. S4 shows only a generic 'strengthening public safety' 2026 priority and S1 shows a prosecutorial career history; neither states an enforcement-vs-prevention position. The -1 is inferred entirely from his resume (former federal prosecutor), which is profile stereotyping, not a policy stance — Democrats of every safety orientation list 'public safety' as a priority. The scorer's own basis concedes 'direction inferred, no explicit enforcement-vs-prevention statement.' Salience is evidenced; direction is not. Drop the score.

Score and reasoning are sound (+1 not +2 correctly accounts for her PCO/DNC/LD-chair insider roles). Flagging the highlights: the endorsements highlight cites only [S5], but The Stranger, Stonewall Democrats, and nursing organizations come from her campaign website S2, and the Planned Parenthood claim from the pamphlet S1; S5 supports only the King County Democrats dual endorsement.

Direction is right but magnitude is exaggerated. The entire evidence is one pamphlet phrase, 'restoring local control' [S1], with zero specifics (no zoning, GMA, schools, or mandate positions). A -2 means fully pole_a; a bare three-word plank supports a lean (-1) at medium (single clear statement), not a full-pole score. Contrast his spending score, where the pamphlet gives multiple concrete elements.

Confidence overstated. The sole evidence is the pamphlet phrase that he will 'back public safety' [S1] — no enforcement content (policing, prosecution, encampments) at all. The scorer's own basis admits 'no specific policy detail'; the enforcement direction is inferred from his party lane and anti-Olympia framing, which is indirect inference — low, not medium.
